text.title: Claude Code Hooks Reference Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: 60s-timeout hook does a multi-API call. Hook times out; tool gets blocked silently.
antipattern.root-cause: Author treated hook as a general automation channel.
antipattern.fix: Move heavy logic to an Agent or external job. Hook should be < 5s.

antipattern.symptom: PostToolUse hook runs `terraform apply`. Tool failed silently mid-edit, half-deployed infra.
antipattern.root-cause: Author thought hooks were a safe place for deploys.
antipattern.fix: Move deploys to a Command + explicit confirmation. Hooks for reversible ops only.

antipattern.symptom: PreToolUse hook returns 1 on block + 1 on transient error. Agent retries blocked tool 3x.
antipattern.fix: Exit 0 allow / 2 block / 1 transient. Document the convention at the top of the script.

antipattern.symptom: Bash blocked by hook; dev has no idea why. Hour-long debug session.
antipattern.root-cause: Author skipped logging to ship faster.
antipattern.fix: Log every block to JSONL with timestamp + tool + args_hash + reason. Add a /tail-hook-log command.
